content:
from Week 5 Discussion
Feb 3, 2025 6:18PM
Ashley Kenny
“A Worn Path” by Eudora Welty is a short story with themes of perseverance and sacrifice. Welty uses symbolism throughout this story to highlight these themes. Phoenix Jackson, the protagonist, is an old African woman who journeys through the woods and fields to search for medication for her sick grandson. During her journey, symbolism is shown to help readers understand her struggles, resilience, and selflessness. Phoenix’s name and the path she follows are two of the most important forms of symbolism found in “A Worn Path”. Her name represents endurance and renewal because it stems from a mythological bird that dies in flames and is reborn from its ashes. Just like the mythological phoenix, she endures similar trials on her journey. She embodies the phoenix’s life cycle because even though she is old, fragile, and has poor eyesight, she endures everything with a certain determination. Phoenix is physically described as “Her eyes were blue with age. Her skin had a pattern all its own of numberless branching wrinkles.”. These details of her appearance let the reader infer that she is fragile and has a deep, rooted history of survival and continues to rise from destruction.
